skill.name: opentui
skill.description: Comprehensive OpenTUI skill for building terminal user interfaces. Covers the core imperative API, React reconciler, and Solid reconciler. Use for any TUI development task including components, layout, keyboard handling, animations, and testing.
skill.summary: Build terminal user interfaces with OpenTUI's core API, React reconciler, or Solid reconciler.
skill.author: msmps

security_audit.summary: After thorough evaluation, the 1896 static analysis findings are overwhelmingly false positives triggered by the static scanner misinterpreting markdown documentation patterns. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings (1547) are markdown code fences, not actual shell execution. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' hits match the word 'hash' or 'md' in technical prose. The 'Keylogger keywords' trigger comes from documentation of legitimate keyboard input handling in a TUI library. No executable malicious code is present; this is a pure reference documentation skill for the OpenTUI library.

low_findings.title: Static scanner false positives on markdown documentation
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged 1896 potential issues across 26 markdown documentation files. Manual review confirmed all are false positives: 'backtick execution' hits are markdown code fences, 'weak crypto' hits are prose mentions, 'keylogger keywords' are legitimate TUI keyboard input documentation, and 'hardcoded URLs' are documentation links. No actual malicious code or intent detected.

content.user_title: Build Terminal UIs Faster with OpenTUI
content.value_statement: Building terminal user interfaces from scratch requires deep knowledge of TUI rendering, keyboard handling, and layout. This skill provides comprehensive OpenTUI reference documentation covering the core API, React reconciler, and Solid reconciler so you can develop TUIs efficiently.
actual_capabilities: Provides reference documentation for OpenTUI's core imperative API, React reconciler, and Solid reconciler
Includes decision trees to help select the right framework and components for a TUI project
Covers component categories: text display, containers, inputs, and code/diff rendering
Documents cross-cutting concepts: layout (Yoga/Flexbox), keyboard input, animations, and testing
Supplies quick start guides, configuration instructions, patterns, and gotchas for each framework
limitations: Documentation only; does not execute or scaffold projects automatically
Requires Bun runtime and Zig for native builds of OpenTUI itself
Some advanced features (native compilation, specific platform support) depend on the underlying OpenTUI version
Does not include interactive examples; reading skill applies documented patterns

use_cases.title: Selecting the right OpenTUI framework
use_cases.description: Use the decision tree to choose between the core imperative API, React reconciler, or Solid reconciler for a new TUI project based on performance and reactivity needs.
use_cases.target_user: Developers starting a new terminal UI project

use_cases.title: Implementing TUI components and layout
use_cases.description: Reference detailed component documentation for text, containers, inputs, code/diff rendering, plus Yoga-based layout patterns to assemble complex terminal interfaces.

use_cases.title: Handling keyboard input and testing TUIs
use_cases.description: Apply documented keyboard input handling, focus management, clipboard support, and testing patterns to build robust, testable terminal applications.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers refining TUI UX and quality

prompt_templates.title: Choose a framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Based on the OpenTUI skill decision tree, recommend which framework (core, React, or Solid) best fits a TUI project that needs fine-grained reactivity and minimal re-renders.

prompt_templates.title: Build a component
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the OpenTUI React reconciler, create a scrollable container that shows a list of selectable items with keyboard navigation. Reference components/containers.md and keyboard/REFERENCE.md.

prompt_templates.title: Configure project
prompt_templates.prompt: Provide the configuration steps to set up a new OpenTUI Solid project with Bun, including tsconfig and bundler requirements from references/solid/configuration.md.

prompt_templates.title: Debug a TUI issue
prompt_templates.prompt: The terminal does not restore correctly after exit. Using core/gotchas.md, identify the likely cause and suggest the correct cleanup pattern.

output_examples.input: Which OpenTUI framework should I use for a high-performance dashboard with frequent updates?
output_examples.output: Choose the Solid reconciler. It provides fine-grained reactivity with optimal re-renders, ideal for dashboards with frequent state changes.

output_examples.input: How do I set up keyboard shortcuts in an OpenTUI app?
output_examples.output: Use renderer.keyInput.on('keypress', handler) to listen for keyboard events. For complex input components, call focus() on the target component to receive keystrokes.

output_examples.input: What build tooling does OpenTUI require?
output_examples.output: OpenTUI runs on Bun. Native code compilation requires Zig. TypeScript changes do not need a rebuild, but native changes do.

best_practices: Always read the framework REFERENCE.md first before diving into api.md or patterns.md
Use renderer.destroy() instead of process.exit() to ensure the terminal is properly restored
Use process.env directly in Bun; do not add the dotenv library
anti_patterns: Calling process.exit() directly in a TUI app, which leaves the terminal in an unclean state
Importing dotenv in a Bun-based OpenTUI project, since Bun auto-loads .env files
Forgetting to focus an input component, which causes it to never receive keyboard events

faq.question: What is OpenTUI?
faq.answer: OpenTUI is a terminal user interface library with a core imperative API plus reconcilers for React and Solid. It runs on Bun and uses Zig for native builds.

faq.question: Which framework should I pick?
faq.answer: Use the core API for maximum control and performance, the React reconciler for familiar component patterns, or the Solid reconciler for fine-grained reactivity and minimal re-renders.

faq.question: Does this skill install or run code?
faq.answer: No. It is reference documentation only. You read the relevant files and apply the patterns to your own OpenTUI project.

faq.question: What runtime does OpenTUI need?
faq.answer: OpenTUI runs on Bun. Native code changes require Zig for compilation; TypeScript changes run directly through Bun.

faq.question: Can I use this with Claude or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is designed to work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as a documentation reference for TUI development tasks.

faq.question: Where are examples and the source library?
faq.answer: The OpenTUI source and examples live at https://github.com/anomalyco/opentui. This skill organizes and summarizes that documentation.
